channel.title: Dan Patrick Show (Filtered)
channel.description: Daily DP Show episodes without C&R or Best Of clips.
channel.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Expensive Tickets, Reggie Miller
item.description:
Tickets for NBA Finals games scheduled at Madison Square Garden are through the roof and a discussion ensues about how much Dan and the Danettes might pay to see their favorite teams play for a championship. NBA legend and Hall of Famer, Reggie Miller joins the show to get everyone ready for tonight’s Game 1 of the NBA Finals.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – Ryen Russillo, Caleb Williams Cursed
item.description:
Longtime friend of the program, Ryen Russillo drops by to weigh in on the NBA Finals which begin tonight. Plus, Dan and the Danettes worry for Caleb Williams future after he was named as the cover athlete for Madden ’27.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – NBA Finals Preview, Aaron Donald’s Rumored Return to the Rams
item.description:
Dan is excited to see how energized New York Knicks fans are about having their squad in the NBA Finals and the buzz surrounding Victor Wembanyama’s debut on the biggest stage the NBA has to offer. And discusses the rumors and the speculation that Aaron Donald is considering a return to the Rams to play alongside Myles Garrett.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Time Will Be Kind to Russell Wilson, Channing Frye
item.description:
The former Seahawks quarterback heads for the broadcast booth and Dan thinks he will be considered for induction into the Hall of Fame in time. NBA champion Channing Frye talks about getting drafted by the Knicks, the atmosphere at Madison Square Garden, and how players have gotten into the mindset for the NBA Finals by hating the other guy’s face.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – Chris Haynes, Russell Wilson Hall of Fame Push
item.description:
NBA insider Chris Haynes joins Dan to discuss how the Spurs fell apart in Game 1, how much they rely on Wembanyama’s performance and courtside altercations involving Jalen Brunson. Russell Wilson retires from football for the broadcast booth and Paulie wants to get the Pro Football Hall of Fame push started early for the former quarterback.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Knicks Steal Game 1, Fan Interference
item.description:
Dan reacts to the Knicks surging back to close and take game 1 in San Antonio. Wemby was great, but the rest of the Spurs cast performs a disappearing act. A fan invades the court and Dan and the Danettes discuss famous fan invasions and why Dan remains worried when this happens so much.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Sam Amick, Prices Still Going Up
item.description:
Chris Mannix takes over for DP today and he checks in with NBA insider Sam Amick about tonight’s Game 2 of the NBA Finals in San Antonio and sparks a discussion about the ridiculous ticket prices for the NBA Finals games coming up in Madison Square Garden.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – Stanley Cup Final Delivers, Jalen Brunson ‘s Legacy
item.description:
Chris Mannix is covering for DP this morning and he weighs in on the overtime thriller last night as the Hurricanes evened up the Stanley Cup Final 1-1, dispatching the Golden Knights. And Chirs and the Danettes open the discussion about Jalen Brunson’s status in the hierarchy of Knicks legends if he were to lead them to a ring.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Must Win for Wemby and the Spurs, Brendan Haywood
item.description:
Chris Mannix is in for DP today and he has the stats to back up his contention that Game 2 is a must-win for the Spurs tonight in the NBA Finals. And former NBA champ and current analyst, Brendan Haywood joins the show to preview tonight’s game.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Ray Ferraro, Reggie Miller
item.description:
Former NHL standout and current analyst, Ray Ferraro joins the show to talk about the Stanley Cup Final and why this season is once again proving that it is the best postseason of any sport on the planet. And Hall of Fame NBA legend Reggie Miller drops by to talk about his experience playing at Madison Square Garden and what we can expect to see tonight in Game 3 of the NBA Finals.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – Don’t Doubt the Knicks, Brendan Sorsby Can Play
item.description:
DP is surprised the point spread for Game 3 of the NBA Finals is so small considering the roll the Knicks have been on during this playoff run. And he addresses the news that Texas Tech QB Brendan Sorsby will be able to play football again because the Texas 99th District Court issued an injunction against the NCAA after they had ruled Sorsby ineligible because of his gambling activities.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Magic Moments at MSG, Ticket Prices for Game 3 Crash
item.description:
Dan talks about the many notable individual performances at Madison Square Garden and thinks Wemby has a chance to cement himself in the history of the game with a standout performance in Game 3 of the NBA Finals tonight. And the tickets prices for tonight have finally fallen but they are still outrageously expensive.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Dan Wetzel, NCAA Handcuffed
item.description:
NCAA insider Dan Wetzel joins the show to break down the Texas 99th District Court injunction ruling that will allow Texas Tech QB Brendan Sorsby to play despite being ruled ineligible by the NCAA over gambling accusations. DP and the Danettes consider what this ruling means for the NCAA moving forward and how it will affect the landscape of college sports.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – Brendan Sorsby Legally Can Play, Christopher “Mad Dog” Russo
item.description:
Texas Tech QB Brendan Sorsby won his legal battle against the NCAA and can play this season after a Texas District Court issued an injunction allowing him to ignore the NCAA ruling him ineligible. And longtime friend of the program, Chris “Mad Dog” Russo stops by to talk about the Madness in Manhattan surrounding last night’s NBA Finals Game 3 at Madison Square Garden.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Spurs Win Game 3, Tim Legler
item.description:
The Spurs took Game 3 of the NBA Finals from the Knicks in Madison Square Garden and DP digs into the action on the court, the drama surrounding the first Finals game at MSG since 1999, and the fallout from the Spurs win. Plus, former NBA player and current analyst calling the Finals for ABC, Tim Legler drops by to weigh in on the spectacle in the Garden last night.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – QB Controversy in Minnesota, World Cup Danettes Draw
item.description:
DP digs into the QB controversy brewing between Kyler Murray and JJ McCarthy at Minnesota Vikings camp. And the crew make their picks for the World Cup which starts tomorrow.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – New York City Is a Powder Keg, Charles Barkley
item.description:
Dan fears for the City of New York as it appears there will be riots in the streets whether the Knicks win or lose the NBA Finals. And NBA legend Charles Barkley drops in to weigh in on violent Knicks fans, Wemby’s non-flagrant foul, and tonight’s Game 4 at the Garden.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Wemby Skates on Flagrant Foul, PJ Carlesimo
item.description:
Dan is convinced the NBA was just trying to make it look good after a secondary review of the play during Game 3 where Wemby made excessive contact with Jalen Brunson determined that a foul should have been called, but it did not rise to the level of a flagrant foul. And former NBA HC PJ Carlesimo joins the show to preview Game 4 of the NBA Finals tonight at Madison Square Garden.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – John McEnroe, Roger Bennett
item.description:
Tennis legend and lifelong Knicks fan, John McEnroe drops in to talk about his experiences last night, courtside at Madison Square Garden.  And soccer analyst Roger Bennett joins the show to talk about the opening of the World Cup and predicts that England will win it all. 

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – Scott Van Pelt, Adam Sandler
item.description:
Sports commentator Scott Van Pelt joined the show to share his thoughts on what happened in last night’s crazy Game 4 of the NBA Finals that led the Spurs to lose the game. And Adam Sandler makes a unscheduled appearance and talks about the unbelievable atmosphere around Madison Square Garden.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Comeback or Collapse…or Both? Vincent Goodwill
item.description:
Dan and the Danettes recaps Game 4 of the NBA Finals with the Knicks pulling out a miraculous win against the Spurs 107-106, despite the Spurs having a 29-point lead for majority of the game. NBA insider Vincent Goodwill joins the show after being in New York for the game.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Kirk Herbstreit, Brad Guzan
item.description:
Former Ohio State quarterback and football commentator Kirk Herbstreit talks about being courtside for the Knicks and trying to make a football parallel to this NBA Finals series, plus weighs in on the continuing issue with Brendan Sorsby at Texas Tech. Former US Men’s National Team goalkeeper Brad Guzan joins Dan to break down the chances for the US team to push for their first World Cup title and the growth of soccer in America.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – Brendan Sorsby Injunction, John Calipari, CC Sabathia
item.description:
Dan weighs on the Brendan Sorsby situation and wonders if Texas Tech is taking this issue seriously enough. Arkansas Head Men’s Basketball Coach John Calipari stops by to talk about the NBA Finals, the scourge of gambling in college basketball and the situation across college sports navigating NIL and recruiting – plus a story about trying to recruit Jalen Brunson. Former MLB pitcher CC Sabathia talks to Dan about whether he’s rooting for the Knicks and whether we’ll see more combo hitter/pitcher players considering Shohei Ohtani’s brilliance.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Stanley Cup Final Bulletin Board Material, US vs. Paraguay, Ernie Johnson
item.description:
Dan reacts to a busy calendar of sports as the Stanley Cup Final and the NBA Finals head toward their completion and the World Cup gets underway. Inside the NBA host Ernie Johnson joins Dan to talk about the NBA Finals and whether Draymond Green has been fired.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Marv Albert, Reggie Miller
item.description:
Longtime Voice of the Knicks Marv Albert reflects on the Knicks' 53-year championship drought ending, and praises Leon Rose for his construction of the roster. Basketball Hall of Famer Reggie Miller praises the Spurs for their postseason run and gives one simple explanation for the Knicks' championship win: Jalen Brunson. 

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – Jalen Brunson’s Legacy, Brendan Sorsby’s Mess Is Getting Worse
item.description:
Dan wants everyone to back off calling Jalen Brunson the greatest Knick of all time after he led them to a title over the weekend. And DP is flabbergasted by the developments currently embroiling the NCAA, Texas Teach and the Big XII over Brendan Sorsby’s status.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Championship Weekend, Jay Wright
item.description:
Dan reacts to both championship crowns that were earned this weekend as the Knicks won their first championship in 53 years over Victor Wembanyama and the San Antonio Spurs, and the Carolina Hurricanes took down the Las Vegas Golden Knights in the Stanley Cup Final. And former Villanova HC Jay Wright stops by to receive his flowers for the "Nova Knicks."

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Mikal Bridges, RIP Eddie Andelman
item.description:
New York Knicks swingman and NBA Champion, Mikal Bridges joins the show to talk about winning a ring and the whirlwind journey he’s been on since Saturday night. And DP pays tribute to legendary sports talk radio host, Eddie Andelman.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – Knicks Parade Projections, Tim Legler
item.description:
Dan is looking forward to seeing the Knicks’ parade on Thursday with estimates upwards of 4 million fans expected to attend. And NBA insider Tim Legler stops by to give his final analysis of the NBA season and the Knicks’ championship.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Cape Verde, Mike Florio
item.description:
DP was captivated by Cape Verde’s performance in their 0-0 draw with powerhouse Spain in their first ever World Cup game and gets a lesson on how to pronounce their country’s name. And NFL insider Mike Florio drops by with details on what it would look like if embattled Texas Tech QB Brendan Sorsby were to declare for the NFL Supplemental Draft as planned.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick
